steps.title: Determine what form is needed
steps.content: The blank completes: "paid to write other ______ but whose names never appear on book covers." Context makes clear ghostwriters are writing stories that belong to other people, and "more than half of all nonfiction books" signals multiple works. The blank needs a plural noun ("stories") with a possessive indicating they belong to "people" ("people's"). Since "people" is already plural, its possessive is "people's" (apostrophe before the s), not "peoples".

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'people’s stories': "people's" is the correct possessive of the irregular plural "people," and "stories" is the correct plural of "story." ✓
  • Choice B — 'peoples story’s': "peoples" lacks the apostrophe needed for possession, and "story's" is a possessive singular, not a plural. ✗
  • Choice C — 'peoples stories': "peoples" without an apostrophe does not show possession; it is not standard English. ✗
  • Choice D — 'people’s story’s': "people's" is correct but "story's" is possessive singular, not the plural "stories" the context requires. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
